column1: #deck:k8s-platform-labs::02-deployments::01-basic-deployment
#separator:Comma
#columns:Front
Does a Deployment ever create or delete a Pod directly?
column2: No — it manages a ReplicaSet, which manages Pods. It's a strict two-hop chain, not one controller doing everything

column1: What is pod-template-hash and where does it come from?
column2: A hash computed from the pod template by the Deployment controller, added to the ReplicaSet's name, selector, and every Pod it creates

column1: Why does a ReplicaSet's selector include pod-template-hash when the Deployment's own selector doesn't?
column2: So the ReplicaSet only claims the Pods it actually created, even though the Deployment's broader selector (e.g. app: nginx) could otherwise match Pods from a different ReplicaSet too

column1: Which controller actually recreates a manually deleted pod under a Deployment?
column2: The ReplicaSet controller, not the Deployment controller — it reconciles actual Pod count against spec.replicas

column1: When would you use matchExpressions instead of matchLabels?
column2: Only when you need NotIn, Exists, or DoesNotExist — matchLabels can't express any of those

column1: Is spec.selector mutable on an existing Deployment?
column2: No — it's immutable, same rule as several Pod fields. Fix is always delete-and-recreate, never patch

column1: What mechanism makes deleting a Deployment cascade to its ReplicaSet and Pods?
column2: Owner references — the ReplicaSet declares the Deployment as its owner, and each Pod declares the ReplicaSet as its owner

column1: What does kubectl scale deployment --replicas=N actually change?
column2: Only the Deployment's spec.replicas — the Deployment controller copies that number to the ReplicaSet, which is what actually creates/deletes Pods

column1: Does replicas: 0 delete the Deployment itself?
column2: No — it deletes all Pods but keeps the Deployment object and configuration intact

column1: What must spec.template.metadata.labels satisfy?
column2: spec.selector — otherwise the Deployment is rejected outright at apply time with a schema validation error

column1: Why does a Deployment use apiVersion: apps/v1 while a Pod uses apiVersion: v1?
column2: Core, foundational types (Pod, Service, Namespace) live in the core/v1 group; controller types that manage other objects (Deployment, ReplicaSet, StatefulSet, DaemonSet) live in the separate apps group

column1: A pod name is nginx-deploy-85f7d4dd78-29r6g. What do the two suffixes mean?
column2: 85f7d4dd78 is the ReplicaSet's pod-template-hash (shared by every sibling pod); 29r6g is a separate random suffix generated per-pod purely for name uniqueness

column1: Does kubectl get all include ConfigMaps, Secrets, or Ingress objects?
column2: No — it only covers a fixed set of common namespaced types (Pods, Services, Deployments, ReplicaSets, etc.); ConfigMaps/Secrets/Ingress/PVCs must be queried individually

column1: Can kubectl logs and kubectl exec target a Deployment directly instead of a specific pod?
column2: Yes — kubectl resolves deployment/name to the ReplicaSet and picks one arbitrary pod from it; useful when you don't care which specific replica, but not for comparing or isolating one pod

column1: Which namespace does the built-in service/kubernetes ClusterIP actually live in?
column2: Only the default namespace — it's not present cluster-wide or in every namespace's kubectl get all output

column1: How does kubectl rollout restart differ from kubectl scale?
column2: rollout restart recreates every existing pod with the same template (same pod-template-hash) via a restart-timestamp annotation; scale only changes the replica count, it never recreates existing pods

column1: If you kubectl scale a Deployment's ReplicaSet directly, does it stick?
column2: No — the Deployment controller reconciles the ReplicaSet's spec.replicas back to match the Deployment's own spec.replicas, typically within seconds

column1: What do the deployment.kubernetes.io/desired-replicas and max-replicas annotations on a ReplicaSet tell you?
column2: They're written by the Deployment controller, not you — max-replicas is spec.replicas plus the rounded-up maxSurge, the same number behind RollingUpdateStrategy's defaults
